hypothesis: Incorporating 3D protein-pocket-derived pharmacophore constraints into the current pharmacophore-guided generative reward (augmenting CATS descriptors with pocket interaction fingerprints) will yield generated compounds that, upon synthesis, exhibit at least a twofold improvement in ERα binding affinity (Kd or IC50) versus compounds produced by the original docking-independent method while maintaining ≥80% novelty (absent from ChEMBL/ZINC/PubChem) and synthetic accessibility scores within ±0.5 of the original method.
